summary: Daniel Okafor and Sofia Petrov met with Harold Jensen to discuss the upcoming renewal of a three-module cybersecurity platform contract (Protect, Detect, and Comply) ahead of an April 30th deadline. Harold raised concerns about a recent six-hour outage that left his manufacturing environment without threat monitoring visibility, and Sofia explained the root cause and architectural fixes implemented since. Harold disclosed exploratory conversations with a competitor (CyberNova) and flagged a gap in CMMC compliance support needed for a Q4 audit. The parties aligned on three key deliverables: an enhanced SLA for Detect, a revised pricing structure accounting for the outage, and a written roadmap commitment on CMMC support.
actionItems: Daniel Okafor: Send revised proposal document by Thursday or Friday including updated SLA language, two-year pricing scenario, and roadmap commitments
Sofia Petrov: Arrange a call between Harold and the Comply product manager to discuss CMMC roadmap timeline
Harold Jensen: Share the proposal document with his legal team and leadership for review
Daniel Okafor: Formalize the enhanced 99.9% Detect uptime SLA with remediation credits in writing as part of the renewal package

keyMoments.text: Harold raises the six-hour outage, noting that going dark on threat detection in a manufacturing OT environment was unacceptable
keyMoments.speaker: Harold Jensen

keyMoments.text: Harold discloses exploratory conversations with competitor CyberNova, driven partly by the outage and partly by product gaps
keyMoments.speaker: Harold Jensen

keyMoments.text: Harold flags that CMMC framework is not covered by the upcoming Comply v2 release, which is a gap given his Q4 audit
keyMoments.speaker: Harold Jensen

keyMoments.text: Daniel offers a formalized enhanced SLA of 99.9% uptime on Detect, up from 99.5%, with remediation credits tied to it
keyMoments.speaker: Daniel Okafor
